sanskrit:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः।
राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः॥
transliteration:
moghāśā moghakarmāṇo moghajñānā vicetasaḥ |
rākṣasīm āsurīṃ caiva prakṛtiṃ mohinīṃ śritāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Baffled hopes, baffled actions, baffled knowledge, bewildered minds—taking refuge in the bewildering demonic and atheistic nature.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that people who intellectually or emotionally reject the Supreme Person’s full reality enter a self-reinforcing loop: their aspirations, efforts, and even their learning repeatedly collapse into frustration because they are aligned with a worldview that denies the very source of coherence and meaning.
psychological_state: Chronic disconfirmation of the need for a transcendent center; resultant grandiosity masking an inner void.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional who mocks religion as ‘opium’ yet feels empty after every milestone, compulsively chasing the next promotion or relationship to fill the hole.
anchor_text: Align your mind with the Supreme to give your hopes, actions, and knowledge lasting meaning.
